content.text:
<session>
I have this TypeScript utility that is not typesafe enough:

function pick(obj, keys) {
    return keys.reduce((acc, key) => {
        if (key in obj) acc[key] = obj[key];
        return acc;
    }, {});
}

function omit(obj, keys) {
    return Object.fromEntries(
        Object.entries(obj).filter(([k]) => !keys.includes(k))
    );
}

function merge(obj1, obj2) {
    return { ...obj1, ...obj2 };
}

Right now these all return `any`. Add proper generic types so that:
- pick({name: 'john', age: 25, role: 'admin'}, ['name', 'age'])
  returns type {name: string, age: number} not {name: any, age: any}
- omit returns the correct remaining type
- merge returns a type that has all keys from both objects,
  with obj2 winning on conflicts
Do not use any external libraries.
</session>

Write the title in the language the user wrote in, regardless of the language of the examples above.

system.text: You are Claude Code, Anthropic's official CLI for Claude.

content.text: {"title":"Add generic types to pick, omit, merge"}
